---
title: "Community"
subtitle: "AI engineering education belongs to everyone."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    css: community.css
---

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/js-yaml@4/dist/js-yaml.min.js"></script>
```

```{=html}
<div class="community-opening">
  <p class="opening-lead">Machine Learning Systems is taught at 40+ universities across six continents — from Bogotá to Johannesburg, from Kobe to Nairobi.</p>
  <p class="opening-body">Every chapter and lab is open and free — and we ship hardware kits to partner universities that need them.</p>
  <p class="opening-principle">AI has no borders. Neither should the education to build it.</p>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<!-- ── The Global Network ────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="network-map-wrap">
  <img src="assets/images/network-map.png"
       alt="World map showing 40+ TinyML4D Academic Network partner universities across North America, Latin America, Europe, Africa, and Asia."
       loading="lazy" />
  <p class="map-caption">The TinyML4D Academic Network — 40+ partner universities teaching ML systems.</p>
</div>

<div class="stats-row">
  <div class="stat"><span class="stat-num" id="cm-stars">22,800+</span><span class="stat-lbl">GitHub Stars</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="stat-num">95+</span><span class="stat-lbl">Contributors</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="stat-num">40+</span><span class="stat-lbl">Universities</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="stat-num">6</span><span class="stat-lbl">Continents</span></div>
</div>

<script>
(async () => {
  try {
    const r = await fetch('https://api.github.com/repos/harvard-edge/cs249r_book');
    if (!r.ok) return;
    const d = await r.json();
    const fmt = n => n >= 1000 ? (n/1000).toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/,'') + 'k+' : n + '+';
    document.getElementById('cm-stars').textContent = fmt(d.stargazers_count);
  } catch(e) {}
})();
</script>
```

```{=html}
<!-- ── Voices from the Community ─────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="spotlight-grid">
  <div class="spotlight">
    <blockquote class="spotlight-quote">
      "This was the first time our students could run a neural network on real hardware they could hold in their hands. That changes how they think about ML — it's not abstract anymore."
    </blockquote>
    <div class="spotlight-attribution">
      <strong>Student voice</strong><br/>TinyML4D partner university
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="spotlight">
    <blockquote class="spotlight-quote">
      "Before this curriculum, our students had no way to learn ML systems with real hardware. The kits and course materials changed what we could teach."
    </blockquote>
    <div class="spotlight-attribution">
      <strong>Marcelo Rovai</strong><br/>Professor, UNIFEI, Brazil
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Beyond the Textbook {#outreach}

Every chapter is free to read — but access means more than a URL. It means hardware, training, and showing up in person.

```{=html}
<div class="outreach-grid">
  <div class="outreach-card">
    <div class="outreach-icon"><i class="fas fa-globe-americas"></i></div>
    <h3>Global Academic Network</h3>
    <p>40+ universities across six continents teaching ML systems — from Harvard and Columbia to institutions across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Through our partnership with the International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP) in Trieste, Italy, we provide curriculum, instructor training, and ongoing support.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outreach-card">
    <div class="outreach-icon"><i class="fas fa-microchip"></i></div>
    <h3>Hardware Where It's Needed</h3>
    <p>We ship real hardware — Arduino and Seeed edge devices — to universities that couldn't otherwise afford them. Every partner institution receives kits so students can learn on real silicon, not just simulations.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outreach-card">
    <div class="outreach-icon"><i class="fas fa-flask"></i></div>
    <h3>Workshops on the Ground</h3>
    <p>The annual SciTinyML workshop series at ICTP in Trieste brings researchers and students together to apply ML to real scientific challenges — from environmental monitoring to health diagnostics in resource-constrained settings.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outreach-card">
    <div class="outreach-icon"><i class="fas fa-book-open"></i></div>
    <h3>Free and Open — Always</h3>
    <p>The full two-volume textbook is available as free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. The HarvardX TinyML Professional Certificate on edX reaches thousands of self-paced learners worldwide. CC-BY-NC-SA licensed so anyone can teach from it.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Workshops & Events {#events}

```{=html}
<!-- Loaded from events.yml — edit that file to add/update events -->
<div id="events-container"></div>

<script>
(async () => {
  const container = document.getElementById('events-container');
  try {
    const resp = await fetch('events.yml');
    if (!resp.ok) throw new Error();
    const data = jsyaml.load(await resp.text());

    const upcoming = data.upcoming.map(e => {
      const tagClass = e.tag === 'Monthly' ? 'tag-monthly' : 'tag-workshop';
      return `
        <div class="event-item">
          <div class="event-date">
            <span class="month">${e.month}</span>
            <span class="day">${e.day}</span>
          </div>
          <div class="event-info">
            <h3>${e.name}</h3>
            <p>${e.location} — ${e.description}</p>
            <span class="event-tag ${tagClass}">${e.tag}</span>
          </div>
        </div>`;
    }).join('');

    const past = data.past.map(e =>
      `<li><span>${e.name} — ${e.location}</span><span class="past-event-year">${e.year}</span></li>`
    ).join('');

    container.innerHTML = `
      <div class="event-list">${upcoming}</div>
      <details class="past-events">
        <summary>Past Events</summary>
        <ul class="past-event-list">${past}</ul>
      </details>`;
  } catch(e) {
    container.innerHTML = '';
  }
})();
</script>
```

## Join Us {#join}

```{=html}
<div class="channel-grid-3">
  <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book/discussions" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="channel-card">
    <span class="channel-icon"><i class="fab fa-github"></i></span>
    <h3>Discussions</h3>
    <p>Ask questions, report issues, and discuss chapters with the community and authors.</p>
  </a>
  <a href="https://mlsysbook.ai/newsletter/" class="channel-card">
    <span class="channel-icon"><i class="fas fa-envelope"></i></span>
    <h3>Newsletter</h3>
    <p>Updates from the author's desk — new chapters, curriculum news, and community spotlights.</p>
  </a>
  <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="channel-card">
    <span class="channel-icon"><i class="fas fa-code-branch"></i></span>
    <h3>Contribute</h3>
    <p>Submit pull requests, improve chapters, build labs, or review content.</p>
  </a>
</div>
```

## Partners & Sponsors {#partners}

The organizations that make this work possible.

```{=html}
<!-- Loaded from partners.yml — edit that file to add/update partners -->
<div id="partners-container"></div>

<script>
(async () => {
  const container = document.getElementById('partners-container');
  try {
    const resp = await fetch('partners.yml');
    if (!resp.ok) throw new Error();
    const data = jsyaml.load(await resp.text());

    const tiers = data.tiers.map(tier => {
      const logos = tier.members.map(m => {
        const link = m.url
          ? `<a href="${m.url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><img src="${m.logo}" alt="${m.name}" loading="lazy" /></a>`
          : `<img src="${m.logo}" alt="${m.name}" loading="lazy" />`;
        return `<div class="partner-logo">${link}</div>`;
      }).join('');
      return `
        <div class="partner-tier">
          <p class="tier-label">${tier.label}</p>
          <div class="partner-strip">${logos}</div>
        </div>`;
    }).join('');

    const cta = data.sponsor_cta
      ? `<p class="sponsor-cta">${data.sponsor_cta} <a href="${data.sponsor_url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Support the project on Open Collective →</a></p>`
      : '';

    container.innerHTML = `<div class="partners-section">${tiers}${cta}</div>`;
  } catch(e) {
    container.innerHTML = '';
  }
})();
</script>
```
